note: Hybrid testing: browser verification via production (stride-five-sigma.vercel.app — has active test session) for checks 1-11 and 19; static source analysis for checks 12-18 and all iter-109 acceptance criteria. Local dev server (localhost:3002) confirmed running but auth cookie injection failed (Supabase SSR uses server-side httpOnly cookies; cross-domain injection not viable). Production is 60+ commits behind current branch but valid for baseline regression checks. All iter-109 changes (api route, types, client wrapper, delete button, canvas link) verified via source analysis as additive-only with zero modifications to existing code paths.

quality_gate.note: Console errors: 0 confirmed via window.__testErrors on production browser session. Network errors: 0 confirmed via window.__networkErrors. Accessibility and layout: no visual anomalies observed in browser snapshots. Login page (localhost:3002): heading, form fields, OAuth button all present with correct roles.

improvements_found.observation: The new Trash2 delete button (lines 207-212 of improvements-view.tsx) has no aria-label. It contains only an SVG icon with no accessible name, so screen readers will announce it as an unlabeled button.
improvements_found.design_principle: WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content — icon-only buttons must have text alternative via aria-label
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Add aria-label='Delete improvement' to the delete button: <button onClick={() => handleDelete(idea)} aria-label='Delete improvement' className='...'>

improvements_found.observation: The IMP-062 fix (target first process-type tab) handles the primary case but has an edge case: if tabs is an empty array, `tabs[0]` in the fallback `?? tabs[0]` would be undefined. The outer `tabs.length > 0` guard prevents the Link from rendering in that case, so there is no runtime crash — but the condition could be slightly cleaner.
improvements_found.design_principle: Code defensibility — guard clauses should be exhaustive
improvements_found.suggested_approach: The existing guard `{tabs.length > 0 && (...)}` already prevents the Link from rendering when tabs is empty, so this is safe as-is. No change needed — noting as an observation only.
